en: Underground work inspections must occur at least daily by a qualified person. Daily inspections by a qualified person must confirm safe equipment operation before use.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 95(2), 98(6)

Only employees who are familiar with the work, physically and mentally fit, and have received special instruction must carry out underground tasks.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 5(4)5
Drilling and securing work must use secure platforms or work baskets.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 95(6)

Workplace ventilation must ensure adequate and consistent airflow in underground areas. Fresh air supply must provide at least 2 m³/min per worker. Ventilation line leak checks and air velocity measurements must occur at least weekly.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 96(1-2)\nExplosive Atmospheres Ordinance, Section 18(1)(1)

Gas concentration monitoring must occur at least daily. Gas measurements must occur after each blast.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 96(5)\nExplosive Atmospheres Ordinance, Section 18(3)(2)

Initial dust measurement must occur after ventilation setup or over a 70 m length. Dust monitoring must generally occur at intervals of no more than two months.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 96(5)
Tunneling dust control must use extraction or spray suppression systems.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 98(8)\nExplosive Atmospheres Ordinance, Section 18(2)
Respiratory protection must be provided and used where exposure limits (e.g., quartz dust) are exceeded.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 96(8)
Motor-driven equipment must include safeguards against unintentional activation.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 98(7)
Traffic routes must provide at least 1.00 m width and 2.00 m height. Non-rail traffic routes must be separated from roadways.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 99(2)

Traffic regulation measures must control movement near conveyors. Vehicle movement near worksites must be limited to walking pace unless otherwise secured.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 99(5), 101(1), 102(3)

Excavation platforms must include two-way communication between the base and the head. Reliable communication must connect underground and surface workplaces.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 104(1), 105(4)
Conveyor systems for persons must include scheduled inspections by qualified persons.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 104(7)
Self-rescue breathing apparatus must be available and clearly marked in tunnels over 500 m.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 105(1)

Escape and rescue planning must cover warning, routes, rescue, and firefighting. Rescue equipment such as stretchers and tools must be available.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 105(2-3), 105(5)
Emergency drills must be conducted at least once annually with documentation.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 105(3)
